data_note: Value analytics sourced from OpenElectricity API (performance_monthly). Pool prices from AEMO MMSDM dispatch_price_daily (Aug 2024 onward). Hourly shape profiles from OpenElectricity 30-min facility data.

pros: Capacity factor trend is improving over time — suggests good maintenance regime or improving local wind conditions
Revenue concentrated in high-price seasons ($85/MWh winter vs $37/MWh summer) — strong seasonal revenue alignment
Large-scale project (530 MW) — benefits from economies of scale in O&M, stronger grid connection and potentially better PPA terms
cons: Low value factor (0.53) — wind is generating heavily during low-price periods, a structural risk that worsens as VIC adds more wind capacity
High year-to-year CF variability (±9.7%) — revenue is harder to forecast, which increases financing risk and PPA pricing margin requirements
Wide intra-year price spread (Oct $26/MWh vs Jun $116/MWh) — revenue is concentrated in a few months, increasing cashflow luminess
Bottom quartile capture price in VIC (#21 of 26 wind farms) — most exposed to cannibalisation in its peer group
